audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Levite substitution and census text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Aaronic line, Nadab/Abihu reminder, Levite service, substitution for firstborn, clan assignments, Levite census, firstborn census, and redemption silver.

verses.restored: These are generations of Aaron and Moses in day YHWH spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
These are names of sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
These are names of sons of Aaron, anointed priests whose hand was filled to serve as priests.
Nadab and Abihu died before YHWH when they brought near unauthorized fire before YHWH in wilderness of Sinai, and sons they had none; Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests before Aaron their father.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Bring tribe of Levi near and stand them before Aaron the priest, and they shall serve him.
They shall keep his charge and charge of all congregation before tent of meeting, to serve service of dwelling-place.
They shall keep all vessels of tent of meeting and charge of children of Israel, to serve service of dwelling-place.
You shall give Levites to Aaron and his sons; given, given they are to him from children of Israel.
Aaron and his sons you shall appoint, and they shall keep their priesthood; outsider who draws near shall die.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Look, I have taken Levites from midst of children of Israel in place of every firstborn opening womb among children of Israel; Levites shall be mine.
For every firstborn is mine. On day I struck every firstborn in land of Egypt, I sanctified to myself every firstborn in Israel, from human to beast; mine they shall be. I am YHWH.
YHWH spoke to Moses in wilderness of Sinai, saying,
Count sons of Levi by houses of fathers, by clans; every male from one month old and upward you shall count.
Moses counted them by mouth of YHWH, as he was commanded.
These were sons of Levi by names: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
These are names of sons of Gershon by clans: Libni and Shimei.
Sons of Kohath by clans: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
Sons of Merari by clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are clans of Levites by houses of fathers.
To Gershon belonged clan of Libnites and clan of Shimeites; these are clans of Gershonites.
Those counted of them by number of every male from one month old and upward were seven thousand five hundred.
Clans of Gershonites shall camp behind dwelling-place westward.
Leader of fathers' house of Gershonites: Eliasaph son of Lael.
Charge of sons of Gershon in tent of meeting: dwelling-place, tent, its covering, screen of entrance of tent of meeting,
hangings of courtyard, screen of entrance of courtyard around dwelling-place and altar, and its cords for all its service.
To Kohath belonged clan of Amramites, Izharites, Hebronites, and Uzzielites; these are clans of Kohathites.
By number of every male from one month old and upward were eight thousand six hundred, keeping charge of holy place.
Clans of sons of Kohath shall camp on side of dwelling-place southward.
Leader of fathers' house of clans of Kohathites: Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Their charge: ark, table, lampstand, altars, vessels of holy place with which they serve, screen, and all its service.
Chief of leaders of Levites: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, oversight of those keeping charge of holy place.
To Merari belonged clan of Mahlites and clan of Mushites; these are clans of Merari.
Those counted of them by number of every male from one month old and upward were six thousand two hundred.
Leader of fathers' house of clans of Merari: Zuriel son of Abihail; they shall camp on side of dwelling-place northward.
Appointed charge of sons of Merari: frames of dwelling-place, its bars, pillars, sockets, all vessels, and all service thereof,
pillars of courtyard around, their sockets, pegs, and cords.

Those camping before dwelling-place eastward, before tent of meeting toward sunrise, were Moses and Aaron and his sons, keeping charge of sanctuary for charge of children of Israel; outsider who draws near shall die.
All counted of Levites, whom Moses and Aaron counted by mouth of YHWH by clans, every male from one month old and upward, were twenty-two thousand.
YHWH said to Moses, Count every firstborn male of children of Israel from one month old and upward, and take number of their names.
You shall take Levites for me—I am YHWH—in place of every firstborn among children of Israel, and beasts of Levites in place of every firstborn among beasts of children of Israel.
Moses counted, as YHWH commanded him, every firstborn among children of Israel.
All firstborn males by number of names from one month old and upward, those counted, were twenty-two thousand two hundred seventy-three.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Take Levites in place of every firstborn among children of Israel, and beasts of Levites in place of their beasts; Levites shall be mine. I am YHWH.
For redemption of two hundred seventy-three firstborn of children of Israel exceeding Levites,
you shall take five shekels apiece by headcount, according to sanctuary shekel; twenty gerahs the shekel.
You shall give silver to Aaron and his sons as redemption of those exceeding among them.
Moses took redemption silver from those exceeding redemption by Levites.
From firstborn of children of Israel he took silver, one thousand three hundred sixty-five shekels, according to sanctuary shekel.
Moses gave redemption silver to Aaron and his sons by mouth of YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.

translation_notes: Levites substitute for firstborn in a ritual-service economy; this is consecrated service, not superiority.
The census includes Levite males from one month old because it concerns substitution/identity, not military readiness.
Clan assignments distribute burdens of transport and care around the dwelling-place.
Redemption silver addresses the numerical excess of firstborn over Levites; it must not be interpreted as pricing human worth.
Nadab and Abihu are recalled as a warning about unauthorized priestly approach, not as permission for fear-based control.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 3 to rank clergy over others, price human lives, or justify lethal gatekeeping. The chapter organizes sacred service and symbolic substitution within ancient Israel.
